item.title: Green Lake Litter Patrol
item.description:
7312 West Green Lake Dr N, Seattle, WA 98103
Sunday, April 19, 2026, 8:45 – 11am

We meet at the Bath House Theater parking lot on the NW corner of Green Lake Park every Sunday at 8:45 am to sign in. All equipment provided. 

We walk the park every Sunday from 9:00am -11:00am. picking up as much litter as we can each week. All are welcome. Litter patrols happen rain or shine. Please dress for the weather & 2+ mile walk.



Event Types: Volunteer/Work Party
Neighborhoods: Ballard, Broadview/Bitter Lake, Citywide, Crown Hill, Fremont, Green Lake, Greenwood/Phinney Ridge, Laurelhurst/Sand Point, Licton Springs, Northgate/Maple Leaf, Wedgwood/View Ridge
Parks: Green Lake Park
Sponsoring Organization: Green Lake Litter patrol
Contact: G Todd Young
Contact Phone: 206-300-1268
Contact Email: gtoddyoung@gmail.com
Audience: Adults, All, Children, Family, Pets, Senior, Special Needs, Teen
Pre-Register: No
Cost: Free

item.title: Volunteer: Clothing Swap Setup, Sort, & Clean-Up
item.description:
Meadowbrook Community Center --Gym
10517 35th Ave. NE
Seattle, WA 98125
Saturday, May 2, 2026, 9:20am – 4:50pm

Volunteer: Clothing Swap Setup & Sort (9:20 a.m.-12:50 p.m.)
We're looking for enthusiastic volunteers to help make our Clothing Swap a success! Roles include- sorting, folding and organizing clothes – Help us keep everything neat and easy to browse. Welcoming Guests – Greet attendees as they arrive and guide them through how the swap works. If you love creating a welcoming environment and enjoy organizing, we’d love to have you on the team! 

Volunteer: Clothing Swap Sort & Clean Up (Noon- 4:00 p.m.)
Roles include- sorting, folding and organizing clothes – Help us keep everything neat and easy to browse. Clean-Up Crew- At the end of the event, help us bag up leftover clothing and prepare items for their next destination.

Your time and energy make this event possible — thank you for being part of it! Reach out to Heather.Wyatt@seatte.gov for more information.

Event Types: Volunteer/Work Party
Building/Room #: Meadowbrook Community Center --Gym 10517 35th Ave. NE Seattle, WA 98125
Neighborhoods: Citywide, Green Lake, Haller Lake, Lake City/Olympic Hills/Victory Heights, Laurelhurst/Sand Point, North of Seattle, Northgate/Maple Leaf, Ravenna/Bryant/Roosevelt, University District, Wallingford, Wedgwood/View Ridge
Location Type: Indoor
Community Center: Meadowbrook Community Center
Contact: Heather.Wyatt@seatte.gov
Contact Phone: 206-684-7522
Audience: Adults, Teen
Pre-Register: No
Cost: Free
More info: anc.apm.activecommunities.com…

item.title: Syttende Mai Parade - Ballard Community Center
item.description:
Ballard Community Center
Sunday, May 17, 2026

Join the Ballard Community Center as we celebrate Syttende Mai (Norwegian Constitution Day)! The parade is from 6-8 pm PST on May 17th 2026! Call the Ballard Community Center for details on volunteering: 206-684-5288

Event Types: Volunteer/Work Party
Neighborhoods: Ballard
Location Type: Outdoor
Community Center: Ballard Community Center
Contact: matt.bourque@seattle.gov
Contact Phone: 2066845288
Site Contact Details: Ballard CC Coordinator: carmen.lau-woo@seattle.gov Assistant Coordinator: matt.bourque@seattle.gov Recreation Leader: chelsea.wong@seattle.gov
Audience: All
Pre-Register: Yes
Cost: Free
More info: www.17thofmay.org

Cost: Free

item.title: Amy Yee (Youth) Tennis Clinics (Free)
item.description:
Amy Yee Tennis Center, 2000 MLK Jr Way S, Seattle, WA 98144
Saturday, June 6, 2026, 8:30 – 11:30am



This is a free 4-week series for youth to 'try' or be introduced to tennis in a fun played based approach. Rackets, etc are all provided for participants and they usually receive a t-shirt. Volunteers do not have to be tennis players, but it's a bonus. Volunteers do everything from gathering balls, 'partnering up' with kids in drills and simply playing in. Volunteers also help set up/break down equipment, etc. The event is hosted if dry. We offer pre-program training for those interested in May, but not required to participate. Volunteers are needed until roughly 1130am, but can stay later for other clinics. Teen volunteers can stay and play during the High School section.

Event Types: Volunteer/Work Party
Building/Room #: Outdoor 6 courts (come prepared for weather)
Neighborhoods: Alki/Admiral, Beacon Hill, Capitol Hill, Central Area/Squire Park, Chinatown-International District, Columbia City, Delridge/North Delridge, Downtown Commercial Core, Duwamish/SODO, First Hill, High Point, Judkins Park, Madison Park, Madrona/Leschi, Miller Park, Mt. Baker/North Rainier, Pioneer Square, Rainier Beach/Othello, Roxhill/Westwood/White Center, Seward Park, South Beacon Hill/New Holly, West of Seattle, West Seattle Junction/Genesee Hill
Location Type: Outdoor
Parks: Amy Yee Tennis Center
Sponsoring Organization: Amy Yee Tennis Foundation
Contact: Carl Bergquist
Contact Email: aytfprogramming@gmail.com
Site Contact Details: Outdoors. Subject to rain cancelation. We notify participants and volunteers if rained out. Note just b/c rain may be happening in the city does not mean the event is canceled. There is parking near the courts.
Audience: Adults, Senior, Teen
Pre-Register: Yes
Cost: Free
More info: amyyee10usfoundation.org

This is a free 4-week series for youth to 'try' or be introduced to tennis in a fun played based approach. Rackets, etc are all provided for participants and they usually receive a t-shirt. Volunteers do not have to be tennis players, but it's a bonus. Volunteers do everything from gathering balls, 'partnering up' with kids in drills and simply playing in. Sponsoring Organization: Amy Yee Tennis Foundation
Contact: Carl Bergquist

item.description:
9536 Ashworth Ave N, Seattle, WA 98103
Saturday, June 13, 2026, 10am – 1pm

item.title: Green Lake Litter Patrol
item.description:
7312 West Green Lake Dr N, Seattle, WA 98103
Sunday, June 14, 2026, 8:45 – 11am

We meet at the Bath House Theater parking lot on the NW corner of Green Lake Park every Sunday at 8:45 am to sign in. All equipment provided. 

We walk the park every Sunday from 9:00am -11:00am. picking up as much litter as we can each week. All are welcome. Litter patrols happen rain or shine. Please dress for the weather & 2+ mile walk.



Event Types: Volunteer/Work Party
Neighborhoods: Ballard, Broadview/Bitter Lake, Citywide, Crown Hill, Fremont, Green Lake, Greenwood/Phinney Ridge, Laurelhurst/Sand Point, Licton Springs, Northgate/Maple Leaf, Wedgwood/View Ridge
Parks: Green Lake Park
Sponsoring Organization: Green Lake Litter patrol
Contact: G Todd Young
Contact Phone: 206-300-1268
Contact Email: gtoddyoung@gmail.com
Audience: Adults, All, Children, Family, Pets, Senior, Special Needs, Teen
Pre-Register: No
Cost: Free

item.title: Friday Lunchtime Litter Pick Up at Seven Hills Park
item.description:
Seven Hills Park
Friday, June 19, 2026, 12 – 1pm

Event Types: Volunteer/Work Party
Neighborhoods: Capitol Hill
Location Type: Outdoor
Parks: Seven Hills Park

Contact Email: timmerkann@gmail.com
Audience: Adults, All, Children, Family, Pets, Senior, Special Needs, Teen
Pre-Register: No
Cost: Free

item.title: Amy Yee (Youth) Tennis Clinics (Free)
item.description:
Amy Yee Tennis Center, 2000 MLK Jr Way S, Seattle, WA 98144
Saturday, June 20, 2026, 8:30 – 11:30am



This is a free 4-week series for youth to 'try' or be introduced to tennis in a fun played based approach. Rackets, etc are all provided for participants and they usually receive a t-shirt. Volunteers do not have to be tennis players, but it's a bonus. Volunteers do everything from gathering balls, 'partnering up' with kids in drills and simply playing in. Volunteers also help set up/break down equipment, etc. The event is hosted if dry. We offer pre-program training for those interested in May, but not required to participate. Volunteers are needed until roughly 1130am, but can stay later for other clinics. Teen volunteers can stay and play during the High School section.

Event Types: Volunteer/Work Party
Building/Room #: Outdoor 6 courts (come prepared for weather)
Neighborhoods: Alki/Admiral, Beacon Hill, Capitol Hill, Central Area/Squire Park, Chinatown-International District, Columbia City, Delridge/North Delridge, Downtown Commercial Core, Duwamish/SODO, First Hill, High Point, Judkins Park, Madison Park, Madrona/Leschi, Miller Park, Mt. Baker/North Rainier, Pioneer Square, Rainier Beach/Othello, Roxhill/Westwood/White Center, Seward Park, South Beacon Hill/New Holly, West of Seattle, West Seattle Junction/Genesee Hill
Location Type: Outdoor
Parks: Amy Yee Tennis Center
Sponsoring Organization: Amy Yee Tennis Foundation
Contact: Carl Bergquist
Contact Email: aytfprogramming@gmail.com
Site Contact Details: Outdoors. Subject to rain cancelation. We notify participants and volunteers if rained out. Note just b/c rain may be happening in the city does not mean the event is canceled. There is parking near the courts.
Audience: Adults, Senior, Teen
Pre-Register: Yes
Cost: Free
More info: amyyee10usfoundation.org

This is a free 4-week series for youth to 'try' or be introduced to tennis in a fun played based approach. Rackets, etc are all provided for participants and they usually receive a t-shirt. Volunteers do not have to be tennis players, but it's a bonus. Volunteers do everything from gathering balls, 'partnering up' with kids in drills and simply playing in. Volunteers are needed until roughly 1130am, but can stay later for other clinics. Teen volunteers can stay and play during the High School section.

item.title: Teen Late Night Tennis
item.description:
Amy Yee Tennis Center, 2000 MLK Jr way S., Seattle, WA 98144
Friday, July 10, 2026, 6:45 – 9:15pm

We are looking for volunteers that are interested in being involved in youth tennis. This Program will focus on ages 12-19. These Friday nights will include lessons, especially for beginners, BUT open to all for a fun Friday night with lots of hitting and fun. Volunteers will be looking to 'hit in' and possibly other tasks.

Event Types: Volunteer/Work Party
Building/Room #: Indoors at AYTC
Neighborhoods: Citywide
Location Type: Indoor
Parks: Amy Yee Tennis Center
Sponsoring Organization: Amy Yee Tennis Foundation
Contact: Carl Bergquist
Contact Email: aytfprogramming@gmail.com
Site Contact Details: runs rain or shine...
Audience: Adults, Senior
Pre-Register: Yes
Cost: Free
More info: amyyee10usfoundation.org
